title: Tafalla bonplandiana
taxonomy: Tafalla bonplandiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. The plant is classified as a perennial herb with small white flowers and is often found in high-altitude regions. Tafalla bonplandiana belongs to the genus Tafalla. The specific classification of Tafalla bonplandiana is not widely documented in botanical sources.

intro: Tafalla bonplandiana, also known as Bonpland's fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Argentina and Uruguay. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, ulcers, and other inflammatory conditions.
